channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: As Trump returns to the White House, families prepare for mass deportations
item.description: For millions in the U.S. illegally or with temporary legal status, the start of Donald Trump’s second term as president comes with a feeling their time in the U.S. may end soon.
item.dc:creator: Gisela Salomon And Sophia Tareen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden urges Americans to 'keep the faith' as he spends final full day as president in South Carolina
item.description: NORTH CHARLESTON, S.C. (AP) — Joe Biden spent his final full day as president Sunday in South Carolina, urging Americans to “keep the faith in a better day to come” and reflecting on the influence of both the civil rights movement and the state itself in his political trajectory.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Melania meme coin launches following Trump's new cryptocurrency
item.description: The website for the coin describes “Melania Memes” as “fungible crypto assets created and tracked on the Solana blockchain.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's inauguration eve rally
item.description: President-elect Trump on Sunday rallied with supporters in Washington, D.C., on the eve of his second inauguration, taking a victory lap and teasing an expected barrage of activity on his first day in office. Here are five takeaways from Trump’s rally at Capital One Arena.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Chicago mayor reiterates opposition to incoming Trump admin's immigration reform
item.description: Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson reiterated his opposition to anticipated immigration reform policies when President-elect Trump returns to the White House this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump to take more than 200 executive actions on day one
item.description: EXCLUSIVE: President Trump will sign more than 200 executive actions on his first day in office—a massive, record-setting first wave of policy priorities focused on border security, energy, reducing the cost of living for American families, ending DEI programs across the federal government and more,
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Capitol Rotunda prepped as inaugural ceremony moves indoors
item.description: The decision Friday to move the ceremony inside forced organizers and security agencies to re-do hundreds of decisions meticulously laid out for months.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE bro Ramaswamy likely to announce Ohio governor run by month's end: sources
item.description: Vivek Ramaswamy, the multimillionaire biotech entrepreneur and former presidential candidate, is likely to launch a bid for Ohio governor by the end of January, sources confirm to Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Immigrants in Chicago and other US cities brace for expected Trump deportation arrests
item.description: Immigrants in Chicago and other U.S. cities have been preparing for immigration arrests since President-elect Donald Trump won the November election.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Tareen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump lays wreath at Tomb of the Unknown Soldier ahead of inauguration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump participated in a wreath-laying ceremony at the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier on Sunday, the eve of his return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Newly built border wall segment further endangers rare desert fish, conservation organization says
item.description: Days before President-elect Donald Trump is sworn into office for a second term, a conservation organization says a newly-built section of border wall harms an endangered fish.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: These notable inaugurations weren't held at the US Capitol. Here's why
item.description: For decades, Americans have gathered at the Capitol Building in Washington D.C. to watch the inauguration of the incoming president, with some noteworthy exceptions.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok says it's restoring service to U.S. users based on Trump's promised executive order
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump says he plans to issue an executive order that would give TikTok’s China-based parent company more time to find an approved buyer before the popular video-sharing platform is subject to a permanent ban in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump wants to visit China, strengthen relationship: Report
item.description: The president-elect has vowed to impose tariffs and other mechanisms against China upon his return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Civil rights leaders and King family mark MLK Day as a special call to action as Trump takes office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When President-elect Donald Trump is sworn in as president of the United States inside the Capitol’s rotunda, he will do so facing a bust of the Rev. Martin Luther King Jr. on the federal holiday commemorating King’s legacy.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden pardons late Black activist Marcus Garvey, 4 others
item.description: President Biden pardoned political activist and Black nationalist Marcus Garvey and four others on Sunday on his last day in office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden's departing UN envoy says US rivals will fill the vacuum if Trump abandons global leadership
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The outgoing U.S. ambassador to the United Nations says she watched America’s leadership diminish in the world during Donald Trump’s first presidency and China fill the vacuum.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judges allow some Capitol riot defendants to return to DC for Trump's inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Thousands of Donald Trump supporters stormed the U.S. Capitol after he lost the 2020 presidential election. Four years later, some of them are allowed to return to the nation's capital so they can celebrate Trump's return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Guard troops will wear a special patch at Trump's inauguration to make it clear they aren't police
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When thousands of National Guard forces and law enforcement officers locked down Washington during racial protests and the Jan. 6 riot four years ago, the blur of camouflage and helmets made it nearly impossible to tell the difference between cops and troops.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ohio Lt. Gov. Jon Husted will succeed JD Vance in the US Senate
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Republican Ohio Lt. Gov. Jon Husted will succeed Vice President-elect JD Vance in the U.S. Senate, the state's governor announced Friday. Gov.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC mayor says his meeting with Trump covered jobs and ceasefire deal, but not his criminal case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams said his meeting with President-elect Donald Trump on Friday included discussions of bringing manufacturing jobs back to the city and the Gaza ceasefire deal — but not the mayor’s federal bribery case.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden sets record by commuting sentences of nearly 2,500 people convicted on nonviolent drug charges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden announced Friday that he was commuting the sentences of almost 2,500 people convicted of nonviolent drug offenses, using his final days in office on a flurry of clemency actions meant to nullify prison terms he deemed too harsh.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Politics Friday: The 2025 Legislature is off to a bumpy start
item.description: DFLers made good on their threat to keep the Minnesota House in limbo earlier this week as both parties fought over control. Republicans pressed forward anyway. MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst and his guests talk about the unconventional start to the 2025 legislative session.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Ramaswamy plans run for Ohio governor
item.description: The Ohio governor’s mansion will be up for grabs in 2026, as sitting Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine (R) is term-limited.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Durbin seeks information from Patel personnel files at national security agencies
item.description: Editor's note: This story has been updated to correct the committee that Durbin leads. Sen. Dick Durbin (Ill.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Senate advances Laken Riley Act, teeing up final vote
item.description: The Senate voted Friday to advance the Laken Riley Act, putting Republicans a step closer to sending the first bill of the 119th Congress to President-elect Trump for his signature next week and giving him an early win on one of his key issues.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Biden says the Equal Rights Amendment should be considered ratified
item.description: President Joe Biden says the Equal Rights Amendment should be considered a ratified addition to the U.S. Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg to host Trump for black-tie reception
item.description: Zuckerberg, along with a collection of pro-sports team owners, is hosting the event on the evening of Inauguration Day.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Biden sets record by commuting sentences of nearly 2,500 people convicted on nonviolent drug charges
item.description: President Joe Biden is commuting the sentences of almost 2,500 people convicted of nonviolent drug offenses. Friday’s clemency actions give Biden the presidential record for most individual pardons and commutations issued.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota courts may get final say in government's constitutional clash
item.description: An ugly opening to Minnesota’s legislative session and a campaign season that won’t end is fueling lawsuit after lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: TikTok ban to impact local businesses, content creators
item.description: San Diego is home to talented and creative content creators who make a living on TikTok. While many are able to make a full-time income on the app, some are also helping small businesses in return, who say the app has inadvertently helped them tremendously.
item.dc:creator: Zara Barker, Fox 5

item.title: Florida's Rubio and Bondi face confirmation hearings
item.description: Former state attorney general and Tampa native Pam Bondi and Senator Marco Rubio are set to appear on Capitol Hill for confirmation hearings, Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC
